pluginDisabled: S&box Auth plugin is not enabled
pluginDisabledDesc: Enable the S&box Auth plugin in project settings to manage game player authentication.

players.kickAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke all active sessions?
players.noPlayersDesc: Players will appear here once they log in via S&box.

settings.serviceNameDesc: The service/game name for Facepunch token validation
settings.sessionTableDesc: Database table to store player sessions
settings.sessionTtlDesc: Session lifetime in minutes. 0 = no expiration
settings.tableNotFound: Table not found or has no columns. Create the table first.

endpoints.title: Protected Endpoints
endpoints.desc: API endpoints using S&box Session authentication
endpoints.noEndpointsDesc: Create an API endpoint with auth type "S&box Session" in the API Builder.

docs.step1: Player joins your S&box game and the client generates an auth token via Sandbox.Services.Auth.GetToken()
docs.step2: Your game sends the token to the /sbox_login endpoint. DataForge validates it with Facepunch API and returns a session_key.
docs.step3: Player uses session_key in the x-session-key header for all subsequent API requests to protected endpoints.
docs.step4: Protected endpoints automatically verify the session and inject {{player_steam_id}} into SQL queries.
docs.apiEndpointsDesc: Built-in endpoints for player authentication. These work automatically when the plugin is enabled.
docs.sboxClientDesc: Example C# code to authenticate from your S&box game
docs.protectEndpointDesc: How to require S&box session auth on your custom endpoints
docs.protectStep1: Go to API Builder and create or edit an endpoint
docs.protectStep2: Set auth type to "S&box Session" — requests without a valid session_key will get 401
docs.protectStep3: Use {{player_steam_id}} in your SQL to scope data to the current player:
